opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't walk straight, my brain is completely blank, and my feet are burning like fire. My husband is calling because I can barely get across the room.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's come on over the last couple of days and it's getting worse tonight — I can't safely get to the toilet on my own and I'm scared of falling.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about two days ago and has been getting steadily worse
symptom_narrative.character: my balance is all over the place, I walk like I'm drunk with my feet wide apart; my head feels totally foggy and blank; and my feet are burning and going numb
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bad enough that I need two people to help me walk — I can't do it on my own
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the unsteadiness is my whole body when I try to walk; the burning and numbness is in both feet, and the numb feeling is creeping up towards my ankles
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time now and worse when I try to stand or move
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting or lying down so I don't have to balance
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up and trying to walk
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no bad stomach upset this time, which is unusual for me; no chest pain, no trouble breathing that I've noticed
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had foggy patches and off-balance days before when I've reacted to gluten, but never this bad and never so fast
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I basically can't walk unaided, I can't concentrate, and I'm terrified of falling in the house tonight

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: gluten problem — I've been told I'm coeliac / very gluten sensitive

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I think I might have eaten something with hidden gluten a couple of days ago at a function; my husband is here with me but he can't physically hold me up if I fall

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says she is very wobbly and walks with her feet wide apart, lurching, and needs two people to steady her when she stands
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: lay description of gait by a family member, not a clinical gait assessment

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says she is alert and awake but slow to answer and keeps losing her train of thought; her face looks even, no drooping, and her speech sounds clear to him
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family member's impression — cannot substitute for a cranial-nerve or neurological examination

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says she doesn't seem feverish or hot to touch, her breathing looks normal and unlaboured, and her colour looks normal
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: no measured temperature, respiratory rate or oximetry available at home

objective_data_offered.value: there are loose rugs on the living-room floor and the bathroom is poorly lit — the patient has to cross both to reach the toilet
